news.headline: WGS Stock Slumps After Hours As Firm Predicts Lower Test Volume For Year
news.summary: GeneDx shares fell after hours after the company signaled softer full-year exome and genome test volume than previously expected. The warning deepened demand concerns first flagged with the Q1 miss and is the freshest negative operational catalyst heading into the Q2 print.

news.headline: GeneDx (NASDAQ:WGS) Downgraded by Zacks Research to "Strong Sell"
news.summary: Zacks Research cut its rating on GeneDx from Hold to Strong Sell in early July, adding to a wave of price-target cuts as analysts reset revenue growth and margin assumptions after the Q1 guidance reset. The downgrade underscores continued sell-side caution even as a few firms like Guggenheim and Piper Sandler stayed constructive.

news.headline: GeneDx price target raised to $63 from $50 at Piper Sandler on coverage policy
news.summary: Piper Sandler lifted its price target to $63 from $50 after Carelon codified outpatient genome coverage, calling it a structural ASP de-risking event. The firm estimates the policy could add roughly $100 of incremental average selling price by the second half of 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

news.headline: GeneDx Names Mark Gardner President to Lead Its Next Phase of Scale and Growth
news.summary: GeneDx appointed Mark Gardner as President, effective June 15, 2026, reporting to CEO Katherine Stueland to lead commercial and operations at scale. The hire, a diagnostics veteran of Quest Diagnostics and Thermo Fisher, came amid the guidance reset and litigation, positioning new management to rebuild execution credibility.

news.headline: Carelon adds outpatient genome sequencing coverage, lifting insured coverage to ~80%
news.summary: Carelon, the largest U.S. lab benefit manager, added genome sequencing under the same clinical criteria as exome for undiagnosed rare disease, raising insured genome coverage to nearly 80%. It is a structural volume and reimbursement tailwind and arguably the most important positive catalyst of the period, underpinning the view that the sell-off went too far.

news.headline: Hedge Funder Eli Casdin Is Loading Up on GeneDx Shares
news.summary: Casdin Capital bought roughly 200,000 more GeneDx shares in early June for about $11.1 million, on top of 500,000 shares bought in May, building a stake of roughly 3.7 million shares as the largest institutional holder. The board-linked specialist's open-market accumulation into the crash is read as a contrarian vote of confidence against the litigation-driven sell-off.

catalysts.label: Warrant expiration (WGSWW)
catalysts.detail: GeneDx's publicly traded warrants (Nasdaq: WGSWW) expire on July 22, 2026, a technical capital-structure event relevant to potential dilution and warrant-related cash inflow.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; guidance calls for Q2 revenue of $110-112 million and ~30,000 exome/genome tests. Market focus is on whether test volume stabilizes after the July 10 warning and whether full-year guidance of $475-490 million holds.

catalysts.label: Reimbursement coverage expansion / ASP uplift
catalysts.detail: Further payer coverage decisions following Carelon's outpatient genome policy could support genome/exome volume and pricing; Piper Sandler estimates roughly $100 of incremental ASP by H2 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

pulse: GeneDx remains headline-driven and high-risk after a Q1 miss, a full-year guidance cut to $475-490 million, and a roughly 49% crash from its early-June record tied to a ~$31 million Fabric Genomics impairment, with shares still under pressure after a July 10 warning of softer full-year test volume and a July 1 Zacks downgrade to Strong Sell. Countering the negative narrative, Carelon's move to cover outpatient genome sequencing (lifting insured coverage to roughly 80%), a Piper Sandler target hike to $63, and specialist Eli Casdin's continued open-market share accumulation offer a bullish counter-case. New President Mark Gardner, on board since mid-June, is now tasked with restoring execution credibility ahead of the early-August Q2 print, while the July 22 warrant expiration remains a near-term capital-structure watch-item.
